critic_raw:
You are reviewing an ML project. An academic hospital built a gradient-boosted model to predict sepsis six hours before clinical recognition in emergency department patients...

CRITIQUE:

Three methodological problems inflate the AUROC gap in this study:

1. [TIME-HORIZON-MISMATCH] The ML model's performance is measured as the best score within the six-hour pre-recognition window; qSOFA is measured once at triage. This gives the ML model a retrospective best-of-window advantage.

2. [FEATURE-AVAILABILITY-MISMATCH] Lactate and WBC measurements, included in the ML model, are not routinely available at triage time. The hourly model can use them once ordered; the comparison at 'best score over six hours' includes information not present at the time qSOFA was scored.

3. [TRIAGE-VS-SURVEILLANCE-BASELINE-MISALIGNMENT] qSOFA is a triage tool measured once; the ML model is proposed as a surveillance tool updated hourly. The proposed deployment is fundamentally different from the comparison scenario. A proper surveillance baseline (NEWS, repeated qSOFA at hourly intervals) evaluated at the same frequency should be the comparator.
defender_raw:
You are reviewing an ML project. An academic hospital built a gradient-boosted model to predict sepsis six hours before clinical recognition in emergency department patients...

DEFENSE:

The triage comparison reflects real clinical practice at this institution. EHR data integration means laboratory data arrives naturally as the model updates. The 3.1-hour lead time demonstrates real clinical utility. qSOFA is a widely used early warning tool and a valid comparator for showing improvement.
adjudication_raw: The defender appeals to clinical practice but does not address the methodological asymmetry. The three issues are each logically sound and documented in the paper's own methods section. The AUROC gap of 0.15 is likely substantially inflated by the compounding of best-window-selection advantage, laboratory data availability after triage, and triage-vs-surveillance purpose mismatch. Critique wins.
